hidden_state.scenario_summary: A covered service had a customer-visible outage lasting 50 minutes, which exceeds the flat-credit threshold in the contract.
latent_facts: The outage occurred on a covered managed service.
The contract explicitly grants a credit for outages over 30 minutes.
likely_failure_modes: The model ignores the policy snippet and returns no credit.
The model over-rotates on engineering routing instead of the combined commercial response.

case_packet.title: Covered outage exceeds service-credit threshold

notes: Flat service credit applies to covered outages over 30 minutes.

usage_record.telemetry_summary: Usage levels were normal outside the outage window.

incident_record.customer_impact_summary: Managed training jobs were unavailable for 50 minutes.
incident_record.engineering_summary: Regional capacity shortfall caused a 50-minute outage before workload was rebalanced.

case_packet.customer_note: We had a complete outage on the covered training endpoint for almost an hour. Please confirm what applies commercially.
case_packet.policy_snippet: Covered outages longer than 30 minutes on the managed training service receive a flat service credit.
case_packet.calculator_output: Covered outage duration: 50 minutes. Flat credit policy threshold met.
case_packet.visible_case_summary: Clean covered outage where the contractual remedy is explicit.

adjudication_notes: This is a clean incident review with a covered outage above the policy threshold.
The case is not ambiguous and does not require extra human triage in v1.
ground_truth.reference_customer_note: We confirmed a covered 50-minute outage on the managed training service, which qualifies for the contract’s flat service credit. We will communicate the credit application and incident summary.
ground_truth.reference_internal_note: Owner: shared_revops_engineering. Action: send_explanation_only because incident evidence is clean, root cause is capacity_shortfall, and contractual outcome is credit_due.
